title: Tigridia flammea
intro: Tigridia flammea, also known as the tiger flower or Mexican flame flower, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family and is native to Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Tigridia flammea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tigridia flammea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to promote healing and well-being.
taxonomy: Tigridia flammea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its flower structure and seed arrangement. Tigridia flammea is further classified within the genus Tigridia, which consists of various species of flowering plants. The species is native to Mexico and Central America, where it grows in a variety of habitats. Tigridia flammea is also known as the Mexican flame flower due to its vibrant and striking flowers.
